content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

November 24, 1992


Diane M. Fishburn
Olson, Connelly, Hagel,
Fong and Leidigh
Capitol Bank Center
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request For Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No. I-92-597

Dear Ms. Fishburn:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/ Because you did not provide
the identity of the person on whose behalf the assistance is being
sought, we are treating your letter as a request for informal
assistance.2/

Your letter, dated October 26, 1992, correctly summarizes my
telephone advice that under the Political Reform Act, the sponsor
of a statewide ballot measure committee which makes contributions
to its sponsored committee during the late contribution period is
not required to file a late contribution report unless the sponsor
otherwise qualifies as a committee. (See Regulation 18419.)


1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Diane M. Fishburn
Our File No. I-92-597
Page Two

If you have any questions regarding this matter, please
contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Alice Hughes
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division

AH:ssa
Enclosures

sections.conclusion: Your letter, dated October 26, 1992, correctly summarizes my telephone advice that under the Political Reform Act, the sponsor of a statewide ballot measure committee which makes contributions to its sponsored committee during the late contribution period is not required to file a late contribution report unless the sponsor otherwise qualifies as a committee. (See Regulation 18419.)
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a sponsor of a statewide ballot measure committee must file a late contribution report when making contributions to its sponsored committee during the late contribution period.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A statewide ballot measure committee sponsor is not required to file a late contribution report when making contributions to its sponsored committee during the late contribution period, unless the sponsor otherwise qualifies as a committee under Regulation 18419.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document formatted as confirmation of prior telephone advice rather than response to formal written question. No distinct QUESTION section header present; question inferred from letter content. Informal advice provided without immunity protections. OCR quality good with minor formatting irregularities.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a sponsor of a statewide ballot measure committee must file a late contribution report when making contributions to its sponsored committee during the late contribution period.

CONCLUSION: Your letter, dated October 26, 1992, correctly summarizes my telephone advice that under the Political Reform Act, the sponsor of a statewide ballot measure committee which makes contributions to its sponsored committee during the late contribution period is not required to file a late contribution report unless the sponsor otherwise qualifies as a committee. (See Regulation 18419.)

embedding.summary: FPPC confirmation of telephone advice regarding campaign disclosure requirements for statewide ballot measure committee sponsors making contributions during the late contribution period. The sponsor is not required to file a late contribution report unless it otherwise qualifies as a committee.
